textByHash.da7674a899f5aa11c5d13a0edd55c864339f925e: I am. Pleased to meet you. I'm assuming you're here because of my advert?
textByHash.8040165b73a0660b2656a2710b56cbf3b9106cd2: Excellent! Let me run you through the details.
textByHash.a42f6c681b310e9917949392fb6de82db4babf6b: We're to head to Elvarg's lair beneath Crandor. There we will begin a research project on Elvarg and her attack on the island.
textByHash.c223e5b71dda7e70b87714be3f6364b3fd0cd3fb: I trust you know the tale of Elvarg?
textByHash.1a97aeff9d842443942f780908a2732f39fbc3df: You're the Hero of Crandor? Amazing!
textByHash.0244cd3c7260be5cf97d96d55e57c1ed30368534: I take it you know her story very well then?
textByHash.8765af7129d0161a5cf942acfa00a1b4404a0dd8: Thirty years ago, an adventurer descended into the caves beneath Crandor. There he discovered the dragon Elvarg.
textByHash.bf8fad440b4bcb24b83d77c5dcfade5742238971: Elvarg made short work of the adventurer. Once she was finished with him, she emerged from the caves and laid waste to the island.
textByHash.f9e77bae7d3d7db5a701555a320741bf1e62a28a: For the next thirty years, Elvarg remained on Crandor until she was finally defeated, by you.
textByHash.ddcafd592777d64168ceb29b7f962fde2901e3db: Now that Elvarg is dead, we have the opportunity to perform research on the island and investigate her attack.
textByHash.f63ce2fdeaa07a3a7d6da8e966d032e154b93806: Meet me in Elvarg's Lair beneath Crandor when you're ready to begin.
textByHash.afb3bfb47b080c9bf044d737bac449ac012610c4: Wonderful. I won't waste your time with it then.
textByHash.f85bdf61ec622f10a565fbd6248e89a2e00d45bd: Elvarg's attack on Crandor is a bit of an anomaly. We're here to work out why.
textByHash.dd77ee885adf91084f32373b1d69def11e4606df: Elvarg is very unusual in that she is one of the few dragons to live alone. Most of her kind live in large groups.
textByHash.30cf63befe55be452436c2c104667daa944a86e1: Not only that, she also displayed far greater aggression than most other dragons by directly attacking Crandor.
textByHash.cde35e42845d6fca16da02a4740f5c0f9f5e6ee2: Not like Elvarg was. Most dragons will only attack those who enter their territory. Elvarg strayed well outside of hers when she attacked the island.
textByHash.1e0a0b41cedf15d37559c56a5c413a98678cf1ba: This is where Elvarg made her home. There must be something here to point us in the right direction. Have a look around and see what you can find.
textByHash.dbfccb06d578ade383c504d133f890b7f8c601c1: Good work. I'm afraid you'll need to be the one to do the mining. My back isn't what it once was.
textByHash.759653db522fa028d68734ea967dce009aaf46e7: Not much to tell really. My name is Dallas Jones and I'm a researcher for the Varrock Museum. Many of the researchers have an area of expertise. Mine is dragons.
textByHash.00b207017a7e718f568f56ba0dbd5ed7e086191b: My father used to live on Crandor before it was destroyed. He was one of the many who were killed by Elvarg.
textByHash.4c96122d568d5d8bb6ed974add2db1ab7878bcd3: His death left me with a great interest in dragons and I've spent the last fifteen years trying to learn more about them.
textByHash.8d0266282b21edf53203fbc968735e5785ec204c: I can see that. We could well be on to something here. You go through the tunnel and see if it's safe. I'll be right behind you.
textByHash.0fcad0fa22f2ce4d3b11542df14437e3ceea75e2: Goodness me! What is this place?
textByHash.204d33370b6b18d748ea4d7082248e8ca0a1eddb: Are the tales true? Could this mean?
textByHash.5867f45ac163016e616a2121436987f11a3b140e: There are ancient tales that tell of the origin of the dragons. They say they were created by a powerful race of creatures known as the dragonkin.
textByHash.41ca47a8d069435ac61ef324b0c5876eec91020c: Yes, you likely will have. We know for a fact that the dragonkin existed. They terrorised the world for many years during the early Fourth Age.
textByHash.f68e833041581549c1ff078a7bf4d938847f4cbf: The thing is, despite their similar names, no actual connection between the dragonkin and the dragons has ever been proven.
textByHash.3ab1038943ea0d2a3dc23e3ffb8b90091ee526ce: We might just be. We should look around and see what we can uncover.
textByHash.93a3a6a78aee72dea3e8442185c5c4d97338b31e: I just can't believe it. This place may not just hold the secrets of Elvarg, but of the whole dragon race!
textByHash.5631daa9a8002ef245bd99500b203602ffcd7879: How strange. We should keep looking around.
textByHash.a505c7fa2f4775a1e68b471b3ccb8f579667b7cd: Lithkren? That name sounds familiar. Where have I seen it before?
textByHash.a911b95c5154c54f17d94c858ad0e5e75fca8cc8: Of course! Fossil Island! I was there recently as part of the research team investigating the differences between wyverns and dragons.
textByHash.2b83c1b36449d0fa2c05f16699fb84b0180919a0: There's an old settlement on the island with similar architecture to this place. It contains lots of books, many written in a strange language. One of the books mentioned a place called Lithkren.
textByHash.14755bd7b95067c7e4023a5c9b8e2c7b7c85363c: Well that just confirms it! Not to mention that Fossil Island is also coated in various types of fungi. It must be connected!
textByHash.8bb0428027cac8f8c4f89ba08637c970613e9887: Fossil Island is also coated in various types of fungi. It must be connected!
textByHash.ca1b0def99e4de84eeeb71619df9c93e11f9c98d: I agree. Head to the old settlement on the north side of the island. I'll meet you there once I've finished up here.
textByHash.ee0eb75d0d3d91f7feac0c80e2724c0483e792c1: It looks like we've found everything here. We should move on to Fossil Island. I'll meet you at the old settlement on the north side of the island.
textByHash.3e611446c1ac71db7fb7e9befb906ac2be94ed63: So while I was waiting for you, I had a look through these books again. One of them mentions a nearby island called Lithkren. Thing is, there are lots of islands around here. It doesn't say which one it is.
textByHash.433d4d133a1f449f189d08115ec39b5561a24f12: Have a look at this grid on the floor. I think it might actually be a map. If you look at that tile in the middle, it looks like part of this island.
textByHash.8bfade34aafadfd644e73d89cfca3d3b0ee33194: That they are. However, I had a look around and I managed to find some of the pieces.
textByHash.d7067adcd981905a5c59f391443a8db50d48c445: By my calculations, there are 24 pieces left to find. I suspect they'll either be in this building or very close by.
textByHash.995fbc12a9eba55fdd5233bfe8afcfd828b47f9f: Good luck. Once you have all of the pieces, bring them here and we'll see about putting this map together.
textByHash.b30ddd24d36b5ab31aeed17db2d4bfa1603f8f3b: Check the pile behind the ladder. You should find it there.
textByHash.3b4445a64033edf5a3391284a217d3732aa6c674: Have you managed to find those map pieces yet?
textByHash.fd615bbbc8351d72d3d2dd34e3f19348b1c7aef0: Well keep looking. We have pieces left to find. I suspect they'll either be in this building or very close by.
textByHash.3eacc43ed57c8d985eb31bd4220a46463a6c19d3: Great. I'll look after them for you.
textByHash.2ea88ae2f4481660aed0e97fcafdab587f4c3438: Not yet. We have pieces left to find. I suspect they'll either be in this building or very close by.
textByHash.0617fd6e36cd71fa316795f5fe88b7016fb27e4e: It looks like it is. Good job.
textByHash.5fbf63c8dc83a848497fdafed30123bc159b0fce: Now then, shall we see about finishing this map?
textByHash.988823a0b7f5070d0d91ab8ecc8eb2d1fdc60786: Fair enough. Let me know when you're ready.
textByHash.a49853c3d07c8a00fb7b7d82b80aa82dc00b0c7f: I do believe you've got it! Yes, we should be able to find Lithkren now.
textByHash.cdffb250328f27cc8ccabe674d6800ea5695d50f: There's a strange symbol on the map in two places. One marks this building and the other marks an island. I bet that's Lithkren.
textByHash.06a0307a1fbe04ab1173adadfee1e046e9a7dc3b: The museum? No that won't work. Any requisition of museum resources requires a mountain of paperwork and we simply don't have time.
textByHash.59ac40a6aadad4d45fd04f26a6c89f7fe4b91229: Of course not! Besides, I've spent years working towards this and I'm tired of waiting. It's time to discover the truth.
textByHash.b568b928275692429ae66df2b386be1bc90bb9b4: We'll need a boat. Find a dwarf called Jardric. He should be somewhere on the island. He's an old friend who owes me a favour. He should be able to get a boat for us.
textByHash.15bbfded80a996c1b55c4deda0910ee256f65dc0: While you're doing that, I'm going to take a copy of this map.
textByHash.b6c3dbbb25cc4e95d719cc22e4fd7f88b90fa2da: We'll need a boat to get to Lithkren. Jardric should be able to help us. See if you can find him. He should be somewhere on the island.
textByHash.c3893032ab33880b06803f20dc40014bad8fa88d: Hello again. How's the boat coming along?
textByHash.bd1b26dbce65365df9caafee07613848398a3ad0: Well talk to Jardric if you need some help. He'll know what to do.
textByHash.a54a509e405543fab0223fbd05ead202e1889d02: This is it. This is our chance to finally find some answers. To finally find out the truth behind Elvarg. Are you ready to go?
textByHash.63b0e667ef69b7d0265a742c920c72c484d489a7: Look at this place! I'm going to explore and see what I can find.
textByHash.abf6d20c19e1560fb64236791f74b39808799503: Well don't take too long. We've had to wait years for this. I don't want to wait much longer.
textByHash.50a43e3d4130f39e818f7806ecc74d43a96102f8: Look at them. Look at the markings on them.
textByHash.0d197ca89f492b8abb8948c2117d768c91271624: Exactly. This is it. This has to be the place.
textByHash.08044cddb8f348e5f66044d29dc715263bc069dd: We can't. I already tried, but the doors are sealed. There's a circular indentation in the middle. Maybe it's a keyhole.
textByHash.f3f8c513f5e13c14205686075e93075f5828bc5f: Well read it and see what it says. It could well contain the information we need.
textByHash.02e41ca8b0f5c3b3d16da4647b322f9ef8d6b26e: Robert the Strong? He spent most of his life fighting the dragonkin. Does this mean it's true? Did they really create the dragons?
textByHash.c95fe1b73423cad01aac3e386ebce82fdb86868e: I guess we'll never know though. Robert the Strong is gone. We've reached a dead end.
textByHash.46583a8361e286cedf73e83b55890be3e590b19b: Just think once before you speak please.
textByHash.d9eab6f97b53f743f143991d6fc52321e91cb6a1: Well I'm definitely not convinced, but let's say you're telling the truth... how can a cat help us?
textByHash.bff82d645598dc8ec5f936641eb2d2f4f5ff6716: This is all very confusing. You go find this cat of yours. I need to sit down and clear my head of all this weirdness.
textByHash.8c0d64c2eeaefca26e5ffa027e0780dda13d7c67: Hello. Have you found that cat yet?
textByHash.c84ee1a9bfa576ebb2b841438e61ca4f6594d9a5: Well keep at it. We need to get through these doors.
textByHash.b4b72a537112bc740d97d67f11ffc563ef25711e: Well no point waiting around. Let's get these doors open.
textByHash.00ed60e082918b20f25572a7c59764cfc27c00a0: I'm Dallas Jones, and I came here for answers.
textByHash.64f4abafba87350ec7cc04ce2737c65d242f4896: I want to know the truth about Elvarg, about Crandor.
